steps.title: Rewrite with common denominator
steps.content:
Notice both sides involve the denominator $(x+5)$. Rewrite the right side with the same denominator:
$$1 - \frac{1}{x+5} = \frac{x+5-1}{x+5} = \frac{x+4}{x+5}$$
The equation becomes:
$$\frac{2(x+1)}{x+5} = \frac{x+4}{x+5}$$

steps.title: Equate numerators and solve
steps.content:
Since the denominators are equal, the numerators must be equal:
$$2(x+1) = x+4$$
$$2x + 2 = x + 4$$
$$x + 2 = 4$$
$$x = 2$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
